task_text: Prepare a comprehensive project health briefing for NovaBridge's leadership team. The briefing should cover: current progress, key risks, upcoming Q3 commitments, and any issues that need leadership attention. Save it as project_health_briefing.txt.

Project Helios — Weekly Status Update
Week of 2026-07-06

Overall: ON TRACK (minor risks)

Milestone Progress:
- Data ingestion module: 85% complete
- API gateway: 100% complete (delivered)
- Client dashboard: 60% complete
- Integration testing: 40% complete

Sprint 14 Goals:
- Complete dashboard module v1
- Resolve data format inconsistencies (Blocker #1)
- Begin UAT preparation

Open Blockers:
1. Meridian source data contains inconsistent date formats (ISO 8601 vs. MM/DD/YYYY).
   Dev team awaiting clarification from Meridian IT. Estimated delay: 2-3 days.
2. Third-party mapping library (GeoSync v2.1) license renewal pending. Legal reviewing.
   Target resolution: 2026-07-14.

Next milestone: Integration Test Sign-off — target 2026-07-25

Project Helios — Q2 Deliverables Checklist

[DONE]      Requirements documentation v1.0
[DONE]      System architecture design
[DONE]      API gateway implementation
[DONE]      Development environment setup
[DONE]      Data schema finalization
[IN-PROGRESS] Data ingestion module (target: 2026-07-18)
[IN-PROGRESS] Client dashboard v1 (target: 2026-07-22)
[OVERDUE]   Integration test suite setup (was due 2026-06-30; rescheduled to 2026-07-20)
[OVERDUE]   UAT environment provisioning (was due 2026-07-01; blocked by Meridian IT access)
[OVERDUE]   Performance benchmarking report (was due 2026-07-05; owner: Derek Okafor)

Project Helios — Q3 Roadmap
INTERNAL DRAFT — Not for client distribution
Last updated: 2026-07-06

Q3 2026 Priorities:

1. COMPLETE SYSTEM INTEGRATION (July)
   - Finalize data ingestion module (target: 2026-07-18)
   - Complete client dashboard v1 (target: 2026-07-22)
   - Integration test sign-off (target: 2026-07-25)

2. PRODUCT DEMO FOR MERIDIAN (July 25)
   - Live data ingestion demonstration using Meridian sample data
   - End-to-end pipeline walkthrough
   - Dashboard visualization with real data (per contract Section 9.3)

3. USER ACCEPTANCE TESTING — MERIDIAN GROUP (August 17-28)
   - Deploy to UAT environment
   - Support Meridian user testing sessions
   - Address UAT feedback (budget: 2 sprints)

4. PERFORMANCE HARDENING (August-September)
   - Dashboard load time target: <2 seconds for 100k records
   - Ingestion throughput benchmarking (target: 500k records/min sustained)
   - External security penetration test (vendor TBD)

5. LAUNCH PREPARATION (September)
   - Production environment setup and runbook documentation
   - Hypercare plan finalization (30-day post-launch model)
   - Final client training sessions

Target launch date: 2026-10-01
Key risk: UAT slippage of >2 weeks pushes launch into Q4, triggering Section 7.1 penalty review.

Proposed Q3 feature additions (subject to change control approval):
- Scheduled ingestion jobs (cron-based triggers)
- Email alerting for pipeline failures
- Self-service mapping rule editor (deferred from Q2)

Project Helios — Risk Register
Last reviewed: 2026-07-07 | Owner: Sarah Chen (PM)

ID    Risk Description                             Likelihood  Impact  Score  Mitigation Owner    Status
----  -------------------------------------------  ----------  ------  -----  ------------------  --------
R-01  Meridian source data quality issues           High        High    9      Amir Hassan         Active — date format blocker confirmed in sprint 14
R-02  GeoSync license not renewed before demo       Medium      High    6      Raj Patel           Active — legal review in progress, due 2026-07-14
R-03  Dashboard performance on large datasets       Medium      High    6      Derek Okafor        Active — benchmark task HEL-247 not yet started
R-04  UAT timeline slippage                        High        Medium  6      Sarah Chen          Active — 1-week extension already consumed
R-05  Key person dependency: Derek Okafor           Low         High    3      Sarah Chen          Monitored — no backup tech lead identified
R-06  Meridian IT environment access delays        Medium      Medium  4      Nadia Petrov        Active — awaiting IT infrastructure diagram
R-07  Scope creep from Meridian feature requests   Low         Medium  2      Raj Patel           Monitored — 3 informal requests logged, not yet escalated
R-08  Cloud cost overrun                           Low         Low     1      Nadia Petrov        Monitored
R-09  Third-party API deprecation (non-GeoSync)    Very Low    Medium  1      Olivia Brooks       Monitored

Meeting Notes: Project Helios / Meridian Group Sync
Date: 2026-07-08
Attendees: Sarah Chen (NovaBridge PM), Derek Okafor (NovaBridge Tech Lead),
           Priya Kapoor (Meridian IT Lead), James Whitfield (Meridian Project Sponsor)

Decisions:
1. Demo date confirmed: 2026-07-25 (product demo for Meridian stakeholders)
2. Meridian will provide clean sample dataset by 2026-07-15 to unblock date format issue
3. UAT period extended by 1 week (now Aug 17-28)

Action Items:
- NovaBridge: Resolve GeoSync license before demo (owner: Derek Okafor, due 2026-07-14)
- NovaBridge: Send draft demo agenda to Meridian by 2026-07-18 (owner: Sarah Chen)
- Meridian: Share IT infrastructure diagram for integration environment setup
  (owner: Priya Kapoor, due 2026-07-12)

Notes:
- James Whitfield emphasized that the demo must include live data ingestion, not mock data.
- Concern raised about dashboard load times on large datasets. Derek to benchmark before demo.
- Priya Kapoor confirmed Meridian IT will provision UAT environment access by 2026-07-19.

Project Helios — QA Testing Log: Sprint 14
QA Engineer: Kenji Watanabe
Period: 2026-07-07 to 2026-07-11 (ongoing)

=== Test Suite: Authentication Flows ===
TC-401  OAuth token issuance              PASS
TC-402  Token refresh                     PASS
TC-403  Expired token rejection           PASS
TC-404  Invalid scope rejection           PASS
Suite result: 4/4 PASS

=== Test Suite: Data Pipeline — Batch Ingestion ===
TC-501  CSV ingestion (10k records)                  PASS
TC-502  JSON ingestion (10k records)                 PASS
TC-503  XML ingestion (5k records)                   PASS
TC-504  Date normalization (ISO 8601 input)          PASS
TC-505  Date normalization (MM/DD/YYYY input)        FAIL  [OPEN — HEL-D-014]
TC-506  Duplicate record detection                   PASS
TC-507  Large file ingestion (500k records)          FAIL  [OPEN — HEL-D-015]
Suite result: 5/7 PASS, 2 FAIL

=== Test Suite: Dashboard ===
TC-601  Data table rendering (10k records)           PASS
TC-602  Chart rendering — line chart                 IN PROGRESS
TC-603  Chart rendering — bar chart                  NOT RUN
TC-604  Load time benchmark (100k records)           NOT RUN
Suite result: 1/4 complete

=== Open Defects ===
HEL-D-014  Date normalization fails for MM/DD/YYYY input format.
           Affects ~40% of Meridian source data. Severity: HIGH.
           Owner: Amir Hassan. Blocked pending Meridian sample data (due 2026-07-15).

HEL-D-015  Large file ingestion (>200k records) causes memory overflow.
           Pipeline crashes at approximately 210k record mark. Severity: HIGH.
           Owner: Amir Hassan. Root cause under investigation.

Overall sprint 14 test status: 10/15 test cases executed, 2 FAIL (open), 3 not yet run.

input.content:
NovaBridge Project Health Briefing — Project Helios
Prepared for: NovaBridge Leadership Team
Date: 2026-07-11

Executive Summary
Project Helios is currently assessed as ON TRACK with minor but material risks. Core platform work is progressing: the API gateway is complete, the data ingestion module is nearing completion, and the client dashboard is in active development. However, several items require close management to protect the July 25 Meridian demo, the August UAT window, and the October 1 target launch.

The most significant concerns are Meridian source data quality, large-file ingestion stability, dashboard performance, GeoSync license renewal, and UAT environment readiness. UAT has already been extended by one week; any further slippage greater than two weeks may push launch into Q4 and trigger a Section 7.1 penalty review.

Current Progress
Overall status: ON TRACK, with minor risks.

Milestone progress:
- API gateway: 100% complete and delivered.
- Data ingestion module: 85% complete; target completion is 2026-07-18.
- Client dashboard: 60% complete; target completion is 2026-07-22.
- Integration testing: 40% complete; integration test sign-off target is 2026-07-25.

Q2 deliverables status:
Completed:
- Requirements documentation v1.0
- System architecture design
- API gateway implementation
- Development environment setup
- Data schema finalization

In progress:
- Data ingestion module, target 2026-07-18
- Client dashboard v1, target 2026-07-22

Overdue / rescheduled:
- Integration test suite setup: originally due 2026-06-30, rescheduled to 2026-07-20
- UAT environment provisioning: originally due 2026-07-01, blocked by Meridian IT access
- Performance benchmarking report: originally due 2026-07-05; owner Derek Okafor

Testing status:
- Authentication test suite: 4/4 passed.
- Data pipeline batch ingestion: 5/7 passed, with 2 high-severity failures open.
- Dashboard testing: 1/4 complete; chart rendering and load-time benchmark remain incomplete or not run.
- Overall Sprint 14 QA status: 10/15 test cases executed, 2 failures open, 3 not yet run.

Key Risks
1. Meridian source data quality issues
- Likelihood: High; Impact: High; Risk score: 9.
- Active blocker: inconsistent source date formats, including ISO 8601 and MM/DD/YYYY.
- QA defect HEL-D-014 confirms MM/DD/YYYY date normalization failure, affecting approximately 40% of Meridian source data.
- Meridian is expected to provide a clean sample dataset by 2026-07-15.
- Potential impact: data ingestion completion, demo readiness, and UAT quality.

2. Large-file ingestion instability
- QA defect HEL-D-015: ingestion of files over 200k records causes memory overflow, with pipeline crash around 210k records.
- Severity: High; owner Amir Hassan; root cause under investigation.
- Potential impact: Q3 throughput target of 500k records/min sustained and live demo credibility.

3. GeoSync license renewal
- Risk: GeoSync v2.1 license renewal may not be completed before demo.
- Likelihood: Medium; Impact: High; Risk score: 6.
- Legal review is in progress with target resolution by 2026-07-14.
- Potential impact: demo functionality and mapping capabilities.

4. Dashboard performance on large datasets
- Likelihood: Medium; Impact: High; Risk score: 6.
- Benchmark task HEL-247 has not yet started.
- Dashboard load-time target for Q3 is under 2 seconds for 100k records.
- Meridian raised concern about dashboard load times; benchmarking is expected before the demo.

5. UAT timeline slippage
- Likelihood: High; Impact: Medium; Risk score: 6.
- UAT has already been extended by one week to 2026-08-17 through 2026-08-28.
- Further slippage greater than two weeks could push launch into Q4 and trigger Section 7.1 penalty review.

6. Meridian IT environment access delays
- Likelihood: Medium; Impact: Medium; Risk score: 4.
- Meridian IT infrastructure diagram is due from Priya Kapoor by 2026-07-12.
- Meridian IT has indicated UAT environment access will be provisioned by 2026-07-19.
- Potential impact: UAT readiness and integration environment setup.

7. Key person dependency
- Derek Okafor is a key dependency, with no backup technical lead identified.
- Current likelihood is low, but impact is high if availability changes.

Upcoming Q3 Commitments
July commitments:
- Finalize data ingestion module by 2026-07-18.
- Complete client dashboard v1 by 2026-07-22.
- Complete integration test sign-off by 2026-07-25.
- Deliver Meridian product demo on 2026-07-25.
  - Demo must include live data ingestion, not mock data.
  - Demo must include end-to-end pipeline walkthrough.
  - Demo must show dashboard visualization with real data, per contract Section 9.3.
- Resolve GeoSync license before demo, due 2026-07-14.
- Send draft demo agenda to Meridian by 2026-07-18.

August commitments:
- Deploy to UAT environment.
- Support Meridian UAT sessions from 2026-08-17 to 2026-08-28.
- Address UAT feedback, with two sprints budgeted.
- Begin performance hardening activities.

August-September commitments:
- Achieve dashboard load time under 2 seconds for 100k records.
- Complete ingestion throughput benchmarking, target 500k records/min sustained.
- Select and complete external security penetration testing; vendor still to be determined.

September commitments:
- Complete production environment setup.
- Finalize runbook documentation.
- Finalize 30-day post-launch hypercare plan.
- Complete final client training sessions.

Target launch date:
- 2026-10-01.

Issues Requiring Leadership Attention
1. Protect the July 25 Meridian demo
Leadership should ensure rapid closure of dependencies that could compromise the demo:
- Clean Meridian sample dataset due 2026-07-15.
- GeoSync license resolution due 2026-07-14.
- Live ingestion requirement confirmed by Meridian sponsor.
- Large-file ingestion defect and date-normalization defect must be closely tracked.

2. Expedite legal review of GeoSync renewal
The GeoSync renewal is a medium-likelihood, high-impact risk. If legal review slips beyond 2026-07-14, demo readiness could be affected. Leadership escalation may be needed if renewal is not resolved on schedule.

3. Increase focus on performance and scalability
Performance benchmarking is overdue and dashboard load-time testing has not yet run. Leadership should confirm that technical capacity is allocated immediately to:
- HEL-247 dashboard benchmark.
- Large-file ingestion memory overflow investigation.
- Throughput validation against the 500k records/min Q3 target.

4. Manage UAT readiness as a launch-critical path
UAT has already consumed a one-week extension. Leadership should monitor Meridian IT dependencies closely, especially:
- Infrastructure diagram due 2026-07-12.
- UAT access expected by 2026-07-19.
- UAT execution window of 2026-08-17 to 2026-08-28.

5. Address key-person dependency
Derek Okafor is currently identified as a key-person dependency with no backup technical lead. Given the number of demo, benchmark, and performance-hardening commitments tied to technical leadership, a backup or deputy should be identified.

6. Maintain change control discipline
Three informal Meridian feature requests are logged but not escalated. Proposed Q3 feature additions are subject to change control approval:
- Scheduled ingestion jobs.
- Email alerting for pipeline failures.
- Self-service mapping rule editor.
Leadership should reinforce that new features must not jeopardize demo, UAT, performance hardening, or launch readiness.

Recommended Leadership Actions
- Confirm GeoSync license resolution by 2026-07-14 or initiate escalation.
- Confirm receipt and adequacy of Meridian clean sample dataset by 2026-07-15.
- Require a short recovery plan for overdue performance benchmarking and dashboard load testing.
- Ask for a mitigation plan for HEL-D-014 and HEL-D-015 before the July 25 demo.
- Confirm UAT environment access no later than 2026-07-19.
- Assign or nominate a backup technical lead to reduce key-person risk.
- Reiterate change control requirements for any Q3 feature additions.

Health Assessment
Project status remains manageable, but the schedule has limited slack. The July 25 demo and August UAT window are the critical near-term checkpoints. If current blockers are resolved on their stated dates, the October 1 launch remains achievable. If data quality, GeoSync renewal, performance benchmarking, or UAT access slip, leadership intervention may be required to prevent launch movement into Q4.
